correct_claim: The corrected formal-kernel H_T scaffold is strong and clears current finite/semi-analytic thresholds; the full H_T theorem remains open pending infinite-basis, domain, and index proofs.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim the no-extra-light-state theorem is proven.
Do not use coordinate-first protected block (0,1,2) as the corrected formal kernel.

limitations: Finite/semi-analytic evidence is strong but is not an infinite-basis theorem.
The full operator domain and topological index theorem remain open.

nodes.statement: Corrected formal-kernel finite/semi-analytic lower bound clears the required threshold.

finite_evidence: formal-kernel convergence scans pass in finite truncations
open_obligations: Prove an infinite-basis uniform complement lower bound independent of k_max.
Prove compactness/relative-bound hypotheses for the complete operator.
nodes.statement: H_perp complement lower bound survives the infinite-basis limit.

finite_evidence: finite matrices are symmetric/Hermitian in the Level 2 scaffold
open_obligations: Prove self-adjointness and domain stability under twist/profile terms.
nodes.statement: The complete H_T operator domain is self-adjoint with the same complement split.

infinite_basis_report.title: BHSM Infinite-Basis H_T Bound Closure Attempt

evidence: Corrected finite Level 2 formal-kernel coordinates are (0,18,36) at k_max=4.
Protected sectors are one lepton, one up, and one down.
Finite formal-kernel scaffold realizes three protected states.
open_obligations: Prove the topological index theorem for the complete twisted Dirac operator.
Exclude mirror zero modes from the full chiral operator, not only the scaffold channels.

remaining_open_nodes: Prove an infinite-basis uniform complement lower bound independent of k_max.
Prove compactness/relative-bound hypotheses for the complete operator.
Prove self-adjointness and domain stability under twist/profile terms.
Prove the topological index theorem for the complete twisted Dirac operator.
Exclude mirror zero modes from the full chiral operator, not only the scaffold channels.
title: BHSM Full H_T Theorem Closure Attempt

v2_1_update.note: v2.1 supersedes the finite-scan perturbation blocker with explicit conditional infinite-basis bounds; formal complement stability remains open.
